title: [Feature]: Evaluate a generic JDBC-style sink framework
problem: Oracle GoldenGate Big Data targets include JDBC. nats-sinks currently uses destination-specific Python connectors and does not expose a generic JDBC sink.
proposal: Research whether nats-sinks should offer a generic JDBC-style sink framework or avoid it in favor of certified destination-specific sinks. The work should evaluate Python JVM bridge risks, driver management, SQL dialect differences, type mapping, idempotency, safe identifier validation, parameterized SQL, and maintenance burden.
users: Operators who need long-tail database targets not covered by a dedicated sink, and maintainers deciding how to scale connector coverage safely.
delivery_semantics: Any JDBC-style sink must return success only after a destination transaction commits. The design must not allow raw SQL templates or unreviewed drivers to weaken injection resistance or idempotency.
security: Dynamic driver loading, connection URLs, SQL templates, credentials, and table identifiers are high-risk. The secure default should be deny-by-default destination allow lists, no arbitrary class loading, and strict parameter binding.
acceptance: Research recommends generic JDBC, destination-specific sinks, or both with clear constraints.
Dynamic driver loading and SQL injection risks are threat-modeled.
No implementation starts without dedicated security review.
Documentation explains why generic database sinks are harder to certify.
tests: Research-only initially. Future implementation requires injection tests, fake driver tests, dialect tests, and sink certification coverage.
documentation: Add JDBC framework findings to sink framework and security documentation.
closeout: Close after the research decision is released.
